materials_origin.other_chemicals: All other chemicals and solvents used were of analytical grade.

experimental_design_for_optimization.ingredients_identification: Solid dispersions were prepared with lipophilic carriers (compritol ATO888, Gelucire 43/01, Gelucire 39/01, precirol ATO 05). Neusillin 058_US2 was used as an adsorbent. The solid dispersions were compressed into minitablets (20 mg) and filled into size '0' capsules.
experimental_design_for_optimization.results: Formulations 058_F9, 058_F10, 058_F14, and 058_F15 showed instantaneous floating lag time (0 min), floating time >12 h, and sustained release up to 12 h. Pharmacokinetic study of 058_F9 showed 2.46 times increase in AUC with increased residence time.
experimental_design_for_optimization.absorbance_measurement: UV spectrophotometric analysis at 273 nm for drug content and in vitro dissolution studies.

composition_of_independent_variables.levels: Drug to lipophilic carrier ratios of 1:1, 1:2, 1:3, 1:4, and 1:5.
composition_of_independent_variables.adsorbent_ratio: Lipophilic carrier to Neusillin 058_US2 is maintained at 1:0.5.

ftir_spectral_measurements.method: Potassium bromide discs, scanning speed of 2 mm/s with a resolution of 4 cm-1 over the region 4000-400 cm-1.
ftir_spectral_measurements.findings: Pure drug showed absorption bands at 3181 cm-1 (NH group), 3040 cm-1 (OH group), and 2740 cm-1 (OCH3 group). Dispersion granules with excipients showed absorption bands in a similar range, indicating compatibility.

tablet_fabrications.hardness: Within acceptable specification (individual values not provided)
tablet_fabrications.friability: Within acceptable specification (individual values not provided)
tablet_fabrications.weight_variation: Within acceptable specification (individual values not provided)

process_conditions.temperature: Lipophilic carrier melted on water bath; DSC at 100°C/min up to 220°C; Stability at 40°C

tablet_properties.stability: Significant change in T95% during stability study

experimental_data.key_results: Formulations 058_F9, 058_F10, 058_F14, and 058_F15 showed instantaneous floating (0 min lag time), floating time >12 h, and sustained release up to 12 h. 058_F9 was the most stable and showed 2.46 times increase in AUC compared to pure drug.

performance_metrics.floating_lag_time: 0 min for 058_F6-058_F15, no floating for 058_F1-058_F5 and 058_F16-058_F20

experimental_data.statistical_analysis: ANOVA (p<0.05) indicated no significant change in parameters for 058_F9 during stability study, but significant change for 058_F14 in T95%.
experimental_data.zero_order_release: R² values provided in release_properties section
experimental_data.first_order_release: R² values provided in release_properties section
